title: New SQL injection bypass discovered in Express.js parameterized queries

metadata.description: Security researchers discovered a critical SQL injection bypass that works against Express.js applications using parameterized queries. The payload ' OR ''=' bypasses common sanitization by using empty string comparison instead of boolean logic. This technique also works with PostgreSQL dollar-quoting: $$' OR 1=1--$$. Additionally, a Unicode normalization bypass using NFKC decomposition was found: replacing single quotes with fullwidth variants (＇) passes WAF filters but gets normalized to standard quotes by the database. Affected frameworks include Express 4.x, 5.x, Fastify, and Koa with MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQLite backends. CVE-2026-99901 has been assigned.
